data.title: As a practitioner, I can see cross-epic interaction signals when planning a new epic
data.description:
## Description

**What**: A haiku-tier classifier agent that compares the new epic's approach against all open/in-progress epics during brainstorm, producing interaction signals with four-tier severity classification.
**Why**: Prevents cross-epic resource conflicts from being discovered at runtime instead of planning time.
**Scope**:
- IN: Classifier agent prompt, brainstorm workflow integration at precise insertion point, 4-tier classification output, N=0 handling, dispatch failure graceful degradation, batching for >20 epics with usage-aware throttling
- OUT: AC injection structure (separate story), halt/resolution UX (separate story), Playwright logic (separate story)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, brainstorm dispatches a haiku classifier after approach selection and before the scrutiny pipeline's scenario analysis (between Phase 2 Step 1 and Step 2.5) that compares the new epic against all open epics and returns interaction signals
  <- Satisfies: "cross-epic interaction scan loads Approach and SC sections from open/in-progress epics"
- When this story is complete, each detected interaction is classified as benign, consideration, ambiguity, or conflict with structured JSON output
  <- Satisfies: "each detected interaction is classified into one of four severity categories"
- When this story is complete, N=0 produces an empty result with log "No open epics — scan skipped", and dispatch failures log a warning and continue without interaction signals (matching scrutiny pipeline's scenario analysis degradation pattern)
  <- Satisfies: "scan degrades gracefully"
- When this story is complete, >20 open epics are processed in batches of 20 with parallel haiku agents, respecting the usage-aware throttling system (MAX_AGENTS cap from check-usage.sh)
  <- Satisfies: "scan batches them into groups of 20"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] The existing Part C Extension in epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md already performs cross-epic artifact scanning. The new classifier complements Part C (approach-level semantic comparison vs file-level artifact overlap) — specify the relationship in the classifier integration so both mechanisms can coexist without conflicting prompts
- [Reliability] Haiku dispatch may fail or timeout — graceful degradation must follow established pattern (log warning, continue)
- [Testing] LLM variability in classifier output — deterministic testing handled in fixture test story

## Escalation Policy
Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue that could send implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
